notes: newfs_hfs does not offer deterministic UUID/timestamp options on macOS. Regenerating via mkhfsplus.sh produces a valid HFS+ volume with the same content structure but different volume UUID and inode creation timestamps — committed .img is a one-time snapshot, not a byte-stable regeneration target.

The HFS+ Private Data directory is created by newfs_hfs on every volume; walker integration tests assert it is NOT enumerated (HfsPlusWalker filters it out by default per SPRINTS_v15 Session C special considerations).

forky.txt has a resource fork created via the macOS named-fork path /file/..namedfork/rsrc. Session D Phase B Part 2 surfaces only the catalog entry; resource-fork exposure as a .rsrc alternate stream is Phase B Part 3 work.
